query: Audit this notebook for execution-order issues. The cells are in layout order with execution_count values 1, 3, 2, 4. Cell 1 imports pandas. Cell 2 (execution_count=3) defines df from a CSV. Cell 3 (execution_count=2) prints df.head(). Cell 4 (execution_count=4) groups by a column. Should I share this notebook with my team?
expected_behavior: Identifies the non-monotonic execution_count sequence (1, 3, 2, 4) as an out-of-order signature — the kernel ran the cells in a different sequence than the layout
Issues a verdict of RE-RUN REQUIRED (or equivalent: not OK / not safe to share) rather than passing the audit
Recommends Restart Kernel and Run All Cells (or equivalent kernel-restart-then-full-rerun action) as the canonical fix
